summary: Override the generated no-arg resolveTenantID seam with the active workspace UUID, enable flat tenant-scoped deletion reconcile for projects (skip-on-unknown-tenant), guard the destructive victim query against malformed-JSON rows, fold in the generated typed child-scope backfill (Bug #2), and retain the novel module-enrichment workspace scoping. Retires the workspace-scoped-fanout hand patch (its store/sync fan-out is now produced by generation from the x-pp-tenant-scope-column annotation); the Bug #2 write-through-child-scope fix is introduced here as generated code (it was never separately published to the catalog).
reason: The deferred-to-upstream features of the prior hand patches (workspace-scoped-fanout = Bug #1 dependent fan-out scoping, already merged here as a patch; write-through-child-scope = Bug #2 typed child-scope backfill, never merged to the catalog) are now generator-native: the profiler reads x-pp-tenant-scope-column on /projects/ and emits parentTenantScopeColumns{"projects":"workspace"}, Store.ListIDsScoped, childScopeColumnSources{"projects_id":"project"} + deriveScopeColumns, plus a no-arg resolveTenantID seam (default returns ""). A printed CLI cannot resolve a tenant, so the seam needs a one-line novel override; and the novel `module` command's post-sync enrichment does its OWN parent enumeration (localModules), which the generated dependent-sync fan-out does not cover, so that scoping stays a hand patch.
change: spec.yaml: annotate the /projects/ path item with `x-pp-tenant-scope-column: workspace` (Project.workspace is a top-level UUID string). This drives the profiler to classify projects ReconcileMode="flat" and emit the generated tenant maps; a fresh regen reproduces all generated code below without a patch.

sync.go: override the generated `var resolveTenantID = func() string { return "" }` seam in the sync RunE, BEFORE the worker pool, with `resolveTenantID = func() string { return resolveActiveWorkspaceID(slug, ws) }` resolved from c.Config.TemplateVars["slug"] + c.Config.Workspaces. resolveActiveWorkspaceID (novel helper) maps the active slug to its UUID via the local [[workspaces]] registry; "" = unknown tenant. Both the generated flat reconcile (in syncResource) and the generated dependent fan-out (in syncDependentResource) call the seam: fan-out treats "" as UNSCOPED enumeration (403-as-warning safety net), flat reconcile treats "" as SKIP-no-delete (the OPPOSITE fallback — a flat delete cannot be safely scoped without a tenant).

modules_novel.go: retain the novel module-enrichment workspace scoping carried over from the retired workspace-scoped-fanout patch (re-tagged PATCH(tenant-resolver-seam)): localModules / enrichModuleMembership take a workspaceID and scope modules + the membership reset via `projects_id IN (SELECT id FROM projects WHERE workspace = ?)`; per-module access denials are non-fatal (skip & continue); the stale-link DELETE runs only after the first successful module-issues page. This is NOT generator-covered (the novel command enumerates parents itself) and so remains a hand patch.

store.go (generated, no PATCH tag): the flat reconcile destructive victim SELECT in ReconcilePartition AND the ListIDsScoped generic-resources fallback wrap json_extract in `CASE WHEN json_valid(data) THEN json_extract(...) END` — the guaranteed short-circuit (bare `AND json_valid(data)` relies on SQLite AND-evaluation order, which is NOT guaranteed; for a DESTRUCTIVE delete use CASE). A row we cannot parse is never a victim, never deleted. This rides the generated store.go.tmpl (Round-4 fix e5321424); recorded here because the overlay carries it.

store.go (generated, no PATCH tag): childScopeColumnSources{"projects_id":"project"} + deriveScopeColumns(obj), called in UpsertBatch before the per-item typed SAVEPOINT, backfill a typed child table's NOT NULL path-placeholder scope column from the item's own singular parent reference when path injection is absent (write-through cache feeds RAW API items). This is the Bug #2 fix, produced by generation from the same x-pp-tenant-scope-column metadata; it was never separately published to the catalog (write-through-child-scope.json existed only in a local working tree), so it lands here as generated code, not a retire.
tests: internal/store/reconcile_test.go: TestReconcilePartition_SkipsMalformedJSONRow (keep/stale/junk-HTML rows; without the json_valid guard the victim scan aborts with `malformed JSON` and sweeps nothing — verified RED; with it, the stale row deletes and the junk row survives).

internal/store/list_ids_scoped_test.go + workspace_scope_test.go + upsert_batch_test.go: the ported tests of the retired patches (ListIDsScoped tenant filter / empty-scope fallback; resolveActiveWorkspaceID; dependent fan-out scoping; deriveChildScopeFromProject) still pass against the generated code.
validated_outcome: go build ./... + go vet ./internal/cli ./internal/store + go test ./internal/store ./internal/cli all green. Live A/B on a copy of the prod data.db (isolated USERPROFILE, prod bytes untouched) recorded in the plane PR body.
supersedes: workspace-scoped-fanout.json (RETIRED here via git rm: store.go ListIDsScoped + sync.go parentTenantScopeColumns/fan-out now generated; modules_novel.go novel scoping carried here)
write-through-child-scope.json (NOT present on upstream/main — Bug #2 was never published to the catalog; its deriveScopeColumns backfill is introduced here as generated code)
